rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes when referring to component ports.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts to avoid type resolution errors.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in appropriate annex scopes: type annexes for component-level assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE contracts are visible in the current AADL scope to prevent resolution failures.
Use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in constant and equation declarations.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers unless explicitly declared in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Guarantee statements must reference valid expressions with known types.
Assignments in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Range assumptions and guarantees should use named constants or visible feature names.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assumptions and bindings there.
Do not reference identifiers or types that are not visible in the current AADL scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
